questions.question: Why is tangent ratio preferred over sine ratio in most height problems?
questions.answer: Tangent ratio is preferred because it does not involve the hypotenuse, which is usually unknown in height problems.

questions.question: Explain why trigonometric ratios are defined only for right-angled triangles.
questions.answer: Trigonometric ratios are based on the ratios of sides of a right-angled triangle and are not valid for other triangles.

questions.question: Why does tan 90° become undefined?
questions.answer: tan 90° becomes undefined because the adjacent side becomes zero, leading to division by zero.

questions.question: Explain why angle of depression is measured from the horizontal.
questions.answer: Angle of depression is measured from the horizontal because the observer’s eye level is considered horizontal.

questions.question: Why is observer height sometimes subtracted instead of added?
questions.answer: Observer height is subtracted when calculating the actual height of the object above ground.

questions.question: Explain the role of alternate interior angles in trigonometric problems.
questions.answer: They help equate angles of elevation and depression when horizontal lines are parallel.

questions.question: Why is trigonometry useful in surveying?
questions.answer: It helps determine heights and distances of inaccessible places accurately.

questions.question: Why is tan 60° greater than tan 30°?
questions.answer: Because tangent value increases as the angle increases between 0° and 90°.

questions.question: Explain why sin 0° is zero.
questions.answer: At 0°, the opposite side is zero, so sin 0° = 0.

questions.question: Why is cos 0° equal to 1?
questions.answer: Because at 0°, the adjacent side is equal to the hypotenuse.

questions.question: Explain why trigonometry cannot be applied directly to obtuse triangles.
questions.answer: Basic trigonometric ratios are defined only for right-angled triangles.

questions.question: Why is tangent ratio called slope?
questions.answer: Because it represents the ratio of vertical rise to horizontal run.

questions.question: Why are trigonometric values constant for a given angle?
questions.answer: Because the ratio of corresponding sides remains constant for similar triangles.

questions.question: Explain why hypotenuse is always the longest side.
questions.answer: Because it lies opposite the right angle, which is the largest angle.

questions.question: Why does tan θ increase as θ approaches 90°?
questions.answer: Because the adjacent side decreases while the opposite side increases.

questions.question: Explain the importance of trigonometry in astronomy.
questions.answer: It is used to calculate distances of stars and planets.

questions.question: Why is angle of elevation measured from eye level?
questions.answer: Because the observer sees the object from eye level.

questions.question: Why is cosine ratio useful in finding distance?
questions.answer: Because it relates the base and hypotenuse of a triangle.

questions.question: Explain why cosec 0° is undefined.
questions.answer: Because sin 0° is zero and division by zero is not possible.

questions.question: Why is trigonometry useful in navigation?
questions.answer: It helps determine directions and distances accurately.

questions.question: Explain why horizontal distance is important in trigonometric calculations.
questions.answer: Horizontal distance is required to apply trigonometric ratios correctly.

questions.question: Why is tan θ preferred in measuring steep hills?
questions.answer: Because it directly gives the slope of the hill.

questions.question: Explain the need for assuming ground as level.
questions.answer: It helps in forming right-angled triangles for calculations.

questions.question: Why are trigonometric ratios independent of triangle size?
questions.answer: Because corresponding sides of similar triangles are in the same ratio.

questions.question: State three real-life applications of trigonometry.
questions.answer: Trigonometry is used in surveying, construction, and astronomy.
